questions.text: What is the difference between Bluetooth and Wi-Fi devices?

options.text: Bluetooth uses 2.4 GHz radio frequency band whereas Wi- Fi can use 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz frequency band
Bluetooth is used for Wireless Local Area Networks (WLAN) only, whereas Wi-Fi is used for Wireless Wide Area Networks (WWAN) only

When information is transmit- ted between two devices using Bluetooth technology, the de- vices have to be in the line of sight of each other. But when Wi-Fi technology is used, the devices need not be in the line of sight of each other
The statement (A) and (B) given above are correct in this context

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A: Bluetooth uses 2.4 GHz radio frequency band whereas Wi- Fi can use 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz frequency band

questions.text: What is ‘Virtual Private Network’?

options.text: It is a private computer network of an organization where the remote users can transmit encrypted information through the server of the organization
It is a computer network across a public Internet that provides users to access their organization’s network while maintaining the security of the information transmitted
It is a computer network in which users can access a shared pool of computing resources through a service provider
None of the statements (A), (B) and (C) given above is a correct description of Virtual Private Network

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option B: It is a computer network across a public Internet that provides users to access their organization’s network while maintaining the security of the information transmitted

questions.text: ‘Project Loon’, sometimes seen in the news, is related to
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option B: Wireless communication technology

questions.text: The web portal DACNET is related to
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A: e-Agriculture

questions.text: The full form of I.B.M. is
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option B: International Business Machine

questions.text: HTML stands for
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option B: Hyper Text Markup Language

questions.text: URL stands for
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option C: Uniform Resource Locator

questions.text: FTP stands for
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: File Transfer Protocol

questions.text: SMPS stands for
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A: Switched Mode Power Supply

questions.text: The full form of UPS is
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A: Uninterrupted Power Supply

questions.text: Which one of the following is not correct statement about ‘Flash memory’?

options.text: It is used for information storage in devices like digital cameras, laptops etc.
It can operate in extended temperature range of – 40ºC to +85ºC
It is cheaper than the normal mechanical disk drives

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option C: It is cheaper than the normal mechanical disk drives

questions.text: The main board of the computer is known as
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option B: Motherboard

questions.text: The first digital computer built with IC chips is known as
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: IBM System/360

questions.text: How many minimum number of NAND gates are needed to implement an Ex-OR functions?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A: 4

questions.text: One of the first viruses to attract widespread public attention was designed to infect MS-DOS on 6th March 1992. Name of the virus is
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option C: Michael Angelo

questions.text: ‘World Computer Literacy Day’ is celebrated on
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A: 2nd December

questions.text: Which of the following is claimed to be the cheapest Tablet P.C. of the world?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A: Aakash

questions.text: Enhancement of the use of information technology for development is the strategy of
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: Andhra Pradesh Government

questions.text: The term Information Technology can be defined as
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: Connectivity + Hardware

questions.text: Computer 1. Is a device capable of storing data. 2. Is capable of analyzing data. 3. Is capable of maintaining total secrecy. 4. Is sometimes attacked by virus. Select the correct answer from the code given below.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: All the four

questions.text: Due to the improper/indiscriminate disposal of old and used computers or their parts, which of the following are released into the environment as e-waste? 1. Beryllium 2. Cadmium 3. Chromium 4. Heptachlor 5. Mercury 6. Lead 7. Plutonium Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option B: 1, 2, 3, 5 and 6

questions.text: With reference to ‘LiFi’, recently in the news, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. It uses light as the medium for high speed data transmission. 2. It is a wireless technology and is several times faster than ‘WiFi’. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option C: Both 1 and 2

questions.text: Computerization of a system needs 1. a will to do it. 2. related financial resources. 3. training of manpower. 4. an ultra modern structure. Select the correct answer from the code given below.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option D: All the four

questions.text: Types of computer memory is/are 1. Semi-conductor 2. Magnetic 3. Server 4. Optical Select the correct answer from the codes given below.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option C: 1, 2 and 4
